name: Battle of AI Test Tools - San Francisco
description: Experience the best AI test tools hands-on—and see how they fit into your testing strategy

sessions.title: Registration & Breakfast
sessions.description: Check-in, grab your badge, and network over breakfast

sessions.title: Welcome
The Great Shift Left: When AI Moves Faster Than Your Roadmap
Quality Engineering in the AI Era: From Test Execution to Trust Building
Testing Starts
Can AI Actually Test Software? Lessons from Real-World Experiments
Are We Testing AI… or Is AI Testing Us?
sessions.description: As AI takes a larger role in generating and executing tests, the traditional boundaries of quality ownership are shifting. This discussion explores trust, accountability, and what it means to validate systems when the logic behind tests is no longer fully human-written or understood.

sessions.title: Flaky Tests vs Flaky AI: Which Problem Is Worse?
sessions.description: Flaky tests have always been a pain—but AI introduces a new kind of unpredictability. Are we actually reducing instability, or just trading one form of flakiness for another? Let’s explore debugging, reliability, and how teams can build confidence in AI-driven testing.

sessions.title: Testing Software That Changes Its Mind: QA in a Non-Deterministic World
What Should Never Be Automated by AI?
sessions.description: AI can generate tests at scale—but should it? This conversation focuses on the boundaries of automation, identifying critical areas where human judgment, domain knowledge, and intuition still play an essential role in ensuring quality.

sessions.title: How Do You Prove ROI for AI in Testing?
sessions.description: Many teams are adopting AI tools without evolving their overall quality strategy. This discussion challenges whether true transformation requires deeper changes in architecture, test design, and team mindset—not just new technology.

sessions.title: Battle Update
Lunch & Networking
sessions.description: Enjoy lunch and connect with fellow attendees

sessions.title: Updates from Judges
Testing Continues

sessions.description: Developers Are Writing Tests. That's Not the Same as Having Quality

sessions.title: Will AI Reduce QA Headcount… or Make QA More Critical Than Ever?
sessions.description: AI is reshaping the role of quality engineers. Will it lead to smaller teams, or elevate QA into a more strategic function? Let’s discuss how roles, skills, and expectations are evolving—and what that means for the future of the profession.

sessions.title: Is Your Test Strategy Ready for AI… or Just Your Tools?
sessions.description: Adopting AI tools is easy—proving their value is not. This discussion focuses on metrics, measurement, and real-world outcomes. What does success actually look like, and how can teams justify continued investment in AI-driven quality initiatives?

sessions.title: AI in the QA Lifecycle - How AI tools can streamline testing processes and introduce autonomous decision-making capabilities
Networking
Selected Team Presentations
Awards & Closing Remarks

speakers.name: Priyamvada Govil
speakers.title: Sr Quality Engineering Manager
speakers.topic: AI in the QA Lifecycle - How AI tools can streamline testing processes and introduce autonomous decision-making capabilities
speakers.bio: Senior Quality Engineering Leader with 10+ years of experience scaling quality for large, complex systems. I lead global quality for 500+ engineers across the org, driving pipeline excellence, automation, performance, and launch readiness. Speaker, mentor, and reviewer in the QA community, with a strong focus on AI-driven testing, scalable quality strategies, and engineering excellence.

speakers.name: Deep Barot
speakers.title: CEO
speakers.topic: Can AI Actually Test Software? Lessons from Real-World Experiments
speakers.bio: Deep Barot is the founder and CEO of ContextQA, pioneering the next generation of software testing through agentic AI and context-aware automation. He’s built a groundbreaking platform powered by 30+ specialized AI agents that autonomously generate, execute, heal, and optimize tests across web, mobile, Salesforce, and API environments. Deep’s vision centers on creating truly intelligent testing systems that understand application context rather than simply executing scripts. This approach has attracted enterprise clients including Airlines, BFSI, and Consumer Tech, who rely on ContextQA’s autonomous agents to deliver unprecedented speed and reliability. Scaling a venture-backed company with a distributed large engineering team, Deep is reimagining how software quality is achieved through multi-agent AI systems that collaborate to solve complex testing challenges—fundamentally transforming how enterprises approach quality assurance in an era of rapid development velocity.

speakers.name: Manju Abraham
speakers.title: Former VP Engineering HPE
speakers.topic: Quality Engineering in the AI Era: From Test Execution to Trust Building
speakers.bio: Manju Abraham is an engineering executive With over 30 years of experience leading global Engineering organizations to deliver enterprise products of high quality, building, scaling and leading transformations, as an effective change catalyst , across Fortune 100  companies like Hewlett Packard Enterprise, NetApp, and HP and startup like Delphix. She was the VP of Engineering for Primary Storage products at HPE leading engineering operations for the business unit of over a few thousand engineers. She brings her passion and energy into these initiatives and has a proven track record as a change agent, transforming organizations and software development lifecycle to improved agility, effectiveness and efficiency; enabling repeatable, predictable, high-quality product releases. She focuses on building relationships to inspire, motivate and bring people across organizations together to execute effectively to the company’s product and business goals. After graduating with a degree in Electronics Engineering, she started her career as a scientist in the Indian Space Research Organization in Bangalore, working on rocket and satellite sensors and payloads. In the USA, she worked on the OS stack, virtualization products and took on engineering leadership roles in HP and Delphix to work on Data Center Automation, Data Virtualization and Data Security Products, and Enterprise Storage products at Netapp and HPE. Manju is passionate about supporting women and diversity and inclusion to help improve the workplace environment. She is actively involved in mentoring, coaching women and advising founders and leaders across the industry.  She is in the board of directors of Women's empowerment and charity non-profits. She lives in the BayArea with her husband and is the proud mother of 2 daughters.

speakers.name: Rashi Agrawal
speakers.title: Head of Agentic AI
speakers.bio: Rashi Agrawal is the Head of Agentic AI at Hinge Health, where she leads the strategic engineering of high-stakes AI systems within complex regulatory landscapes. By expertly navigating the rigorous requirements of clinical safety, HIPAA, and global regulations, she ensures that disruptive technology remains both secure and compliant. Focused on the pioneer side of generative technology, Rashi is architecting "state-of-the-art" frameworks for Agentic AI that move beyond simple automation to solve critical member problems and drive dramatic business growth. Formerly the Head of AI at GoodLeap, a leading FinTech in Green Energy, Rashi spearheaded enterprise-wide AI transformation initiatives that optimized everything from complex loan processing to customer engagement while managing a complex cross functional global portfolio. With a unique blend of holistic vision and deep technical expertise, she has a proven track record of developing intelligent decision-making systems and risk assessment platforms that deliver measurable business value through Generative and Agentic AI. Earlier in her career, Rashi led engineering teams at Yahoo, where she transformed early-stage technical challenges into massive growth engines for their multi billion-dollar  advertising business. Her approach is defined by a commitment to technical innovation grounded in strategic business transformation, ensuring that AI serves as a competitive moat rather than just a technical layer. Beyond the office, Rashi is a global explorer who has traveled to over 45 countries, bringing a diverse, international perspective to her leadership. She is an active thought leader in the Engineering Leadership and AI community. An Indian immigrant with a Master’s in Computer Science from San Jose State University, she is also the founder of Women In Tech AI (WIT AI), a community dedicated to empowering and elevating women leaders in the artificial intelligence space.

speakers.name: Vasusen Patil
speakers.title: Technical CEO
speakers.topic: Testing Software That Changes Its Mind: QA in a Non-Deterministic World
speakers.bio: Vasusen Patil is the co-founder and CEO of Donobu, an AI-powered QA platform. It helps QA teams author, run, and maintain end-to-end Playwright tests, so they can focus on test strategy and coverage instead of fighting flaky scripts. Previously, as Senior Director of Engineering at Coursera, he led the consumer engineering org through 10x revenue growth to nearly $700M and an IPO. As an early engineer at WePay (YC09), he built the payments APIs that powered platforms like GoFundMe. He has spent his career making sure the products people depend on actually work.

speakers.name: Frederick Goris
speakers.title: Founding Engineer
speakers.topic: Testing Software That Changes Its Mind: QA in a Non-Deterministic World

judges.name: Tejash Patel
judges.title: Head of Quality Engineering
judges.bio: Tejash Patel is a Senior Quality Engineering leader with 20+ years of experience driving SDLC excellence across streaming, cybersecurity, and cloud-based platforms. At Prime Video Advertising, he leads quality for large-scale ad experiences, building and guiding globally distributed teams to deliver reliable, low-latency video and live sports at massive scale. Previously at McAfee, he led kernel- and endpoint-level quality for enterprise EDR/MDR/XDR solutions, improving time‑to‑market through deep automation and CI/CD integration. Tejash is an author and practitioner of AI-driven and agentic testing, applying multi-agent workflows for scenario expansion, risk-based regression, and defect analysis in production environments. He has judged industry awards such as the Globee Awards for Cybersecurity and Technology; and the Technovation for Girls Awards. Now, he serves as a judge for Battle of the AI Test Tools, bringing a practitioner-first, outcomes-driven lens to evaluating modern testing tools.
